mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions, lowers the equity discount rate, and lifts S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because then rate cuts are interpreted as support rather than distress.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 25 bps over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show higher 20d SPX returns than non-triggered observations, or if the return premium disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is rising faster than 0.2 pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer demand and revenue growth expectations, which supports S&P 500 earnings revisions and positive forward returns over the next week. The effect is weakest when retail sales growth is flat or negative because the market then lacks confirmation of demand momentum.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth rises above its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS growth surges above the trigger but SPX 5d forward returns are negative more often than positive, or if the sign of the effect is unstable across rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening labor income and consumption, which reduces earnings expectations and raises recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is most reliable when unemployment increases persist for several months rather than as a one-off print.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20d forward returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if the trigger frequency is followed by positive returns in more than 60% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates broad output expansion and improving utilization, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when industrial production growth is driven by transient restocking rather than final-demand strength.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth is positive and above its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO growth does not correspond to above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the sign turns negative during periods when industrial growth is broad-based and persistent.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST indicates softer residential investment and slower wealth effects, which feeds into weaker cyclical demand and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when housing starts are falling while the labor market is still stable, because that pattern isolates demand slowdown from broader recession panic.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% versus its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the effect vanishes when UNRATE is stable within 0.1 pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A high or rising FEDFUNDS rate tightens discount rates and credit conditions, compressing equity multiples and reducing S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal becomes actionable only when policy is already restrictive enough to slow risk appetite rather than when rates are still near the effective lower bound.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 2.0% and has risen in each of the last 2 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-rate triggers do not coincide with lower 5d SPX returns than baseline, or if the sign is positive in a majority of tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor-market weakening is confirmed across both employment creation and joblessness, signaling lower income growth and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The joint condition should outperform either series alone because it filters out noise from temporary payroll volatility.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth turns negative while UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the combined trigger does not produce lower 60d SPX returns than either PAYEMS or UNRATE used separately, or if the combined signal is not negative in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in INDPRO and PAYEMS indicates synchronized supply and labor expansion, which supports earnings breadth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. This regime-specific signal is strongest in recoveries because rising production and hiring then reflect durable reacceleration rather than late-cycle overheating.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent INDPRO and PAYEMS gains do not outperform baseline 20d SPX returns in recovery periods, or if the joint effect disappears when either series is declining.
